text.title: decision-analysis Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for decision-analysis as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Analysis performed to justify a decision already made: options are scored after the winner is known, and weights are set to produce the predetermined result.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reviewers detect post-hoc rationalization from weight patterns that perfectly match the chosen option. Trust drops and the decision process loses legitimacy for future decisions.

failure-mode.description: Pure financial trade-off scored on a 1-5 scale instead of using NPV or discounted cash flow.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A "cost" score of 3 vs 4 throws away precision that financial analysis preserves. For purely financial decisions, use NPV directly; reserve 1-5 scoring for non-quantifiable criteria.

failure-mode.description: Agents will hallucinate vendor capabilities and pricing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force evidence URLs on every cell and reject cells without primary sources (vendor docs, pricing pages, GitHub repos), not blog posts.

failure-mode.description: LLMs cluster in the middle of 1-5 scales (regression to median).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Calibrate by asking for percentile rank instead, or by anchoring with two extreme reference options.

failure-mode.description: A single agent doing all scoring inherits its own biases across the whole matrix.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use parallel scoring agents (one per criterion or one per option) and compare; large disagreement = low confidence cells.

failure-mode.description: Agents will silently mix scoring directions ("cost: 5 = expensive" in one row, "5 = cheap" in another).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fix at the schema level: every criterion has an explicit `direction: "higher_better"|"lower_better"`.

failure-mode.description: Date drift: pricing and uptime data goes stale fast.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Stamp `retrieved_at` on every fact; agents must refuse to reuse evidence > 90 days old without re-fetching.

failure-mode.description: LLMs love to recommend the option you mentioned first or last.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Randomize option order in the prompt before the score pass.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The matrix is decision *support*, not decision *making*. Human-in-the-loop checkpoints: weights (before scoring), final rec (before commitment), post-implementation calibration review.

failure-mode.description: Watch for prompt-injected criteria: if option descriptions come from vendor websites, vendors can plant criteria favoring themselves.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Strip suspicious instructions; never let vendor copy define weights.
